text: Petra. Hebrew selah, "the rock." (Haydock)<hr>Our Saviour sprung from Ruth, the Moabitess. (Menochius)<hr>The original may insinuate, that the king of the country had neglected to pay the usual tribute to Juda, 4 Kings 3:4. (Calmet)<hr>"Send the lamb to the ruler," etc. (Tournemine.) Amid scenes of distress, the prophet perceives that the Saviour will proceed from one of this nation. (Worthington)
Arnon. They shall not be able to fly over, or to escape the conqueror.
Night. Seek a retreat in the darkest places; or protect Israel when they shall flee before the Assyrians. Their cruelty is thus insinuated, Amos i.
Dust. Theglathphalassar. I need not exhort you to receive my people, as I know your dispositions, and they are out of danger. (Calmet)
Just. This regards Christ, (St. Jerome) prefigured by (Haydock) Ezechias. (Calmet)
Lords. Princes of Jerusalem, (Lamentations 1:1.) or of Assyria. (Calmet)<hr>Sea. Of Sodom, even as far as Jazer, (Haydock) in the tribe of Ruben. (Calmet)

My tears. Isaias 15:5. (Haydock)<hr>I announce a different sort of music from that which is customary in times of harvest, and of vintage. The liquor shall be tears, Isaias 63:2., and Jeremias 48:32, 33. (Calmet)
Carmel. This name is often taken to signify a fair and fruitful hill or field, such as Mount Carmel is. (Challoner)<hr>It means, "the vine of God." (Calmet)
Wall. Kir-hareseth, ver. 7. I am grieved at your misfortunes. (Calmet)
Prevail. Chamos shall not be able to help them.
That time. A long while ago, Psalm 92:2.

Not many. It was laid waste in the third year of Ezechias. But its final destruction took place only five years after that of Jerusalem. (Calmet)<hr>The wars against Moab continued three years, after which it was reduced to servitude. (Worthington)
